title: Awesomely Optimized
description: Same look, same game, double the frames!
body:
Amazingly Optimised is a modpack which focuses on pure performance, with absolutely **0** extra bloat!

It is incredibly simple to install new mods to enchance your experience.
If you need ideas for mods to download, I highly recommend this video by Salc1:
<iframe width="460" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/pTc09RtJRZY" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>
(also check the pinned comment for some extra suggestions)

# Features

### Insane FPS gains
In my testing, the mods in this modpack raised the FPS by over **2.5X**!
![insane fps gains](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/4fa50f6275d4158ab6b950ea9e462d3af5461130_0.webp)
<small>tested on 1.21.11 Fabric</small>


### Easy to add mods
Thousands of mods are avaliable to download at the click of a button. From a minimap, to hundreds of new biomes, the possibilities are **ENDLESS**!

### Speedy updates
My aim is to try and update the modpack as quick as possible to newer (and sometimes older) versions.

![Update All](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/5de9af2cc818a6c31fd03884478e90952fc798a5.png)

### Release channels
Each version of a modpack has one of three channels (Release, Beta, and Alpha).
Release - Fully (or 99%) complete with all the mods needed for optimisation.
Beta - Typically for new versions of the game when some of the mods havent updated yet.
Alpha - Typically for snapshots, or very new versions (will probably never use this one).

### Can I use this in my modpack?
Of course you can. Anyone is free to use my modpack to make their own modpacks have the best peformance possible, but may require some tweaks to get it working properly. You don't need to give me credits, but they are appriciated :D

Also, I will not (usually) update any versions that are older than the current version with new mods. Here are some that I recommend that may not be in your version:

Concurrent Chunk Management Engine (C2ME) - Fabric/Quilt https://modrinth.com/mod/c2me-fabric, NeoForge https://modrinth.com/mod/c2me-neoforge, Forge https://modrinth.com/mod/c2mef - spreads the Minecraft workload across all your CPUs cores and threads. **NOT RECOMMENDED** if your CPU has less than 4 cores (not included in any versions of the modpack)
